sections.paragraph:
adj. [pl. const. דַּכְּאֵי], intensive from the root דָּכָא (of the form קַטָּל)-

(1) very much crushed, broken very small, hence as a subst. that which is very small, poet. for dust. (Arab. دَكَّاءُ dust [rejected in Thes.].) Psa 90:3, תָּשֵׁב אֱנֹושׁ עַד־דַּכָּא “thou turnest man to dust.”

(2) broken in spirit, cast down, Isa 57:15 Psa 34:19.
